title: achillea tenuifolia
intro: Achillea tenuifolia, also known as yarrow, is a perennial herb belonging to the aster family, native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its fern-like foliage and white or yellow composite flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating menstrual cramps. The health benefits of yarrow include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce stress and anxiety, and act as a natural diuretic. Yarrow can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used topically as a poultice or ointment to promote healing and reduce inflammation.
